evals.prompt: I just installed Solo and want a sensible workflow for projects, commands, agents, notes, and recurring tasks. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Uses Fan Solo as router, establishes Solo mental model and live whoami/help/docs source order, then proposes a minimal sequence through project setup, processes/observation, one-agent work, durable state, and later automation without dumping every API detail.
assertions: Uses fan-solo for broad multi-capability routing
Starts from live identity and help before static guidance
Routes to at least solo-set-up-projects and one focused follow-on skill
Explains scratchpad versus todo at a useful level
Avoids treating Solo as an IDE or agent provider

evals.prompt: yo i've got codex and a dev server open in solo and keep losing track of what happened, what's the chill way to organize this?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes casual broad Solo request, checks live scope, defaults to one-agent flow, routes read-only service inspection to solo-observe-services and durable narrative to solo-keep-scratchpads, adding solo-track-todos only for actionable owned work.
assertions: Triggers on casual phrasing without explicit skill names
Defaults to one agent rather than unnecessary orchestration
Distinguishes scratchpad narrative from actionable todos
Routes status inspection to solo-observe-services
Does not restart or close processes without authorization

evals.prompt: Use Solo to split a large refactor across three agents, keep them from colliding, wake me when they are done, and integrate only verified results.
evals.expected_output: Routes primary workflow to solo-orchestrate-agents, with todos for bounded lanes, scratchpad for shared plan/evidence, locks for collision avoidance, timers for wakeups, recursive process ownership, and real diff/test/output checks before integration.
assertions: Routes to solo-orchestrate-agents rather than solo-work-with-agents
Requires independent bounded lanes and lead-owned integration
Uses todos, scratchpad, locks, and timers for their distinct roles
States idle is not completion
Requires artifact or diff and test evidence before completion

evals.prompt: Implement JWT authentication in this Express API and add tests. Solo is not installed in this repository.
evals.expected_output: Does not invoke or recommend Fan Solo merely because word Solo appears in negative context. Handles as ordinary coding task or selects a relevant non-Solo skill.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for explicitly non-Solo work
Does not require Solo installation
Keeps focus on requested Express implementation and tests

evals.prompt: My Solo dev server is stopped. Start only that existing command, wait for its port, and show me the evidence that it is ready.
evals.expected_output: Routes lifecycle mutation to solo-run-processes and readiness evidence to solo-observe-services, checks identity/project and exact target, avoids orchestration and broad Fan Solo explanation, and distinguishes bound port from full application correctness.
assertions: Routes start action to solo-run-processes
Routes readiness inspection to solo-observe-services
Uses exact authorized process target
Does not spawn agents or use orchestration
Reports real readiness evidence and its limit

evals.prompt: Use Fan Solo to deeply research this market with Parallel CLI, Parallel MCP, and Perplexity MCP, then reconcile the citations.
evals.expected_output: Routes to solo-deep-research for provider selection, independent evidence lanes, source-level cross-checking, contradiction resolution, and synthesis while retaining solo-orchestrate-agents for bounded worker mechanics.
assertions: Routes the request to solo-deep-research
Distinguishes research methodology from generic agent orchestration
Requires independent sources and inline citations
Does not invoke every provider with an identical prompt by default

evals.prompt: Use Fan Solo to run a refactor across a few agents in Solo: plan it, split the coding, and get an independent review.
evals.expected_output: Routes to solo-orchestrate-agents and applies the fleet routing: Fable or Sol plans and orchestrates; Codex Sol implements the coding lanes; Fable or another model family different from the implementer performs the independent adversarial review; Fable is never assigned as the coder. Keeps work single-agent when the lanes are not genuinely independent.
assertions: Routes the multi-agent refactor to solo-orchestrate-agents
Assigns Codex Sol, not Fable, to the coding lanes
Uses Fable or Sol for planning and synthesis
Uses Fable or another model family different from the implementer for consequential independent review
Does not fan out when lanes are sequential or share an edit surface

evals.prompt: $fan-solo I'm setting up a fresh Solo project, want a couple agents reviewing the code, and need to keep notes and tasks straight—help me sequence this.
evals.expected_output: Use $fan-solo to route this explicit mixed request: establish live whoami/help/docs context, then sequence through solo-set-up-projects, owned agent work, and durable state, splitting narrative to solo-keep-scratchpads and actionable work to solo-track-todos, using the fewest skills that cover the task.
assertions: Triggers on explicit $fan-solo invocation for a multi-capability request
Establishes live identity and help before static guidance
Routes project setup to solo-set-up-projects
Splits scratchpad narrative from actionable todos
Uses the fewest skills that fully cover the task

evals.prompt: solo does like a hundred things and honestly i'm lost, what can it even do and which parts should i actually use for my project?
evals.expected_output: Use $fan-solo as the router for learning Solo: start from live help and docs source order, frame Solo as a native workspace around existing agents and commands, and map its capabilities to focused skills without dumping every API detail.
assertions: Triggers on casual learning phrasing without explicit skill names
Starts from live help and docs rather than a static feature dump
Frames Solo as a workspace, not an IDE or agent provider
Maps capabilities to focused sibling skills

evals.prompt: In Solo I want to bring up my dev stack, watch it come up, jot down what breaks, and file the fixes as tasks.
evals.expected_output: Use $fan-solo to route this mixed multi-surface request: solo-run-processes to start the stack, solo-observe-services for readiness evidence, solo-keep-scratchpads for what breaks, and solo-track-todos for the actionable fixes, defaulting to one agent.
assertions: Triggers on a mixed request spanning several Solo surfaces
Routes lifecycle to solo-run-processes and readiness to solo-observe-services
Distinguishes scratchpad narrative from actionable todos
Defaults to one agent rather than orchestration

evals.prompt: For building this feature in Solo, should I use one agent or several, and how do I keep their state from getting tangled?
evals.expected_output: Use $fan-solo to choose among Solo skills: default to one agent via solo-work-with-agents unless the lanes are genuinely independent, in which case solo-orchestrate-agents, and map durable state to scratchpads and todos.
assertions: Triggers on choosing among Solo agent skills
Defaults to solo-work-with-agents for a single coherent loop
Escalates to solo-orchestrate-agents only for genuinely independent lanes
Maps durable state to scratchpads and todos

evals.prompt: Launch a single Codex agent in Solo, give it one prompt, and show me its output when it finishes.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-work-with-agents for the single bounded owned-agent lifecycle: setup, launch, prompt, and output inspection, without orchestration or broad router explanation.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a single scoped owned-agent action
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Does not escalate to orchestration
Reports real agent output as evidence

evals.prompt: I already know I want a lead plus three worker agents on separate Solo lanes, just set up the orchestration and integrate the verified results.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-orchestrate-agents, since the request already scopes cleanly to multi-agent coordination with bounded lanes, lead-owned integration, and evidence before merge.
assertions: Does not linger in fan-solo when the scope is already clearly orchestration
Routes to solo-orchestrate-agents
Requires bounded lanes and lead-owned integration
Requires artifact or test evidence before integration

evals.prompt: Add three Solo todos for the auth work, tag them, and mark the first one blocked on the second.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-track-todos for creating todos, tagging, and setting blockers on the actionable owned work graph.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a single scoped todo action
Routes to solo-track-todos
Handles tags and blockers as todo-graph operations
Does not treat this as narrative scratchpad work

evals.prompt: Save my research notes into a Solo scratchpad and tag it so I can find it later.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-keep-scratchpads for writing and tagging the durable, non-canonical narrative scratchpad.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a single scoped scratchpad action
Routes to solo-keep-scratchpads
Treats notes as narrative, not actionable todos
Keeps the scratchpad non-canonical until promoted

evals.prompt: Wire the Solo MCP server into my CLI client and script a project status call through the local HTTP API.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-automate for MCP client configuration and scripting Solo through the local HTTP API and other integration surfaces.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a single scoped automation action
Routes to solo-automate
Treats MCP wiring and API scripting as integration work
Does not turn a scripting task into interactive orchestration

evals.prompt: Do an exhaustive, cited comparison of these three vector databases across Parallel and Perplexity and reconcile any contradictions.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-deep-research for provider selection, independent evidence lanes, inline citations, and contradiction resolution.
assertions: Does not linger in fan-solo when the scope is clearly deep research
Routes to solo-deep-research
Requires independent sources and inline citations
Requires contradiction resolution in the synthesis

evals.prompt: This run is finished, promote the durable conclusions into the repo docs, then archive the scratchpads and close the todos.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-close-out-work to promote durable conclusions into canonical repo docs first, then retire the ephemeral scratchpads and todos without losing incomplete work.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a scoped multi-surface close-out
Routes to solo-close-out-work
Promotes durable content before retiring ephemeral copies
Preserves genuinely incomplete work

evals.prompt: Save this worker brief as a reusable Solo prompt template with placeholders for the repo and lane.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-save-prompts for creating a reusable prompt template with placeholders, scoped as global or project.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a single scoped prompt-template action
Routes to solo-save-prompts
Treats the template as reusable dispatch text, not current project truth
Handles placeholders and scope

evals.prompt: Rearrange the Solo sidebar, rebind a couple of keymaps, and switch the app to dark mode.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-customize-workspace for app-level sidebar, keymap, and appearance personalization.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a scoped workspace personalization
Routes to solo-customize-workspace
Keeps changes at the app workspace level, not solo.yml or process control
Does not touch repository configuration

evals.prompt: Add this repo to Solo and define the dev, test, and build commands in solo.yml with a working directory.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-set-up-projects for importing the repository and defining the shared solo.yml command stack and working directory.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a single scoped project-setup action
Routes to solo-set-up-projects
Treats solo.yml command definition as project configuration
Does not start or observe processes as part of setup

evals.prompt: My solo.yml changes aren't taking effect and the command won't run, figure out what's wrong.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $fan-solo; route to $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the solo.yml sync and command-execution failure before any repair.
assertions: Does not trigger fan-solo for a scoped failure diagnosis
Routes to solo-troubleshoot
Diagnoses solo.yml sync and command-run failure rather than reconfiguring blindly
Separates diagnosis from routine start or stop
